location.office_time_required: three days per week in-person; travel to client sites approximately 1-2 times per week within continental US

level_signals: 4+ years of client facing experience required
$165,000 OTE in SF/NYC consistent with mid-senior individual contributor account manager
Owns relationships with largest and most strategic customers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven ability to build and manage existing customer account relationships
Proven ability to establish and optimize operational processes related to customer account management
Strong customer success skills and demonstrated ability to work through difficult conversations to preserve relationships
Excellent communication, listening, facilitation, and negotiating skills
Strong data analysis, organization, and prioritization skills
Ambitious, detail-oriented and self-starter with a proven track record of delivering results
A quantitative aptitude and ability to present to clients with sophisticated investment knowledge and business experience
Experience working cross-functionally across multiple teams and demonstrated ability to drive projects to completion
Ability to travel both locally and on extended trips within the continental US to visit clients on-site and in-person approximately 1-2 times per week
Strong expectation is placed on in-person presence of three days per week to build trusted relationships and deliver best-in-class service.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Prior experience working in venture capital,  private equity or accounting strongly preferred

scope.surface: Carta's venture capital and fund administration platform; account management for largest and most strategic customers
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fund administration services for private capital markets
core_responsibilities: Develop and own deep relationships with largest and most strategic customers to drive product adoption and long-term success
Partner closely with customers to create and manage tailored project plans that align with their business goals
Design and execute strategies to increase customer engagement and reduce churn
Lead customers through the renewal process, including direct conversations around scope changes, service escalations, and contract terms
Negotiate renewal agreements focused on creating mutually beneficial outcomes
Monitor product usage, customer sentiment, and engagement trends
Identify new opportunities for customers to deepen their use of Carta products and services
Identify and activate high-potential customers to serve as references and advocates

compensation.equity_form: equity for all full time roles

green_flags: Equity included for all full-time roles
Clear role scope and responsibilities outlined
Carta is a well-known, well-funded private markets platform with recognizable brand (Fortune, Forbes, Fast Company citations)
Company serves 65,000+ companies across 160+ countries — significant scale
Role owns strategic, high-value customer relationships — meaningful business impact
red_flags: Only OTE figure disclosed, no base/commission split stated — unclear how $165K is structured
Heavy travel requirement (1-2 client site visits per week) plus 3 days/week in-office — high time commitment on location
Role is 'Other' archetype (account manager/CS), not a PM or growth product role, despite being posted in an ATS context
Salary listed only for SF and NY — unclear comp for other locations despite mention of 'hiring for multiple levels and locations'
